description:
Moku is a fast, minimal manga reader frontend for Suwayomi-Server.


Moku is a fast, minimal manga reader frontend for Suwayomi-Server.

It wraps Suwayomi's GraphQL API in a lightweight Tauri app — no Electron overhead.
# Moku v0.9.3
**Released:** May 16, 2026
> This MAY be a stable build, but may still contain bugs. If you only wish to read and not test, proceed with caution.

## New Features
- **Minimize to System Tray** — Moku can now minimize to the system tray when closing, with multimodal window management (#76)
- **Auto-Scroll** — Auto-scroll support for long strips and webtoons, with double-tap adjustment (#69)
- **Middle-Click Browser Auto-Scroll** — Middle-click now toggles browser-style auto-scroll in the reader (#70)
- **Extension Settings** — Per-extension settings now available (#71)
- **Library Filtering** — Filter library by source/extension, with series count per extension (#72, #73)
- **Bulk Source Migration** — Migrate series between sources in bulk from the series detail page (#66)
- **Backtracing & NavPage Store** — Improved navigation history and page state management
- **Circular Page Loaders** — Loading indicators added to pages (#79)

## Bug Fixes
- Fixed window dragging being blocked by the reader bar (#74)
- Fixed folder scrolling in the library (#75)
- Fixed toolbar uniformity and series detail redirect (#66)
- Fixed library folder drag in the toolbar
- Fixed library folder settings and clipping issues
- Fixed reader page-order respect during loading and memory eviction (#61, #63, #68)
- Fixed performance settings render limit CSS (#63)
- Fixed scroll propagation in the reader (#63)
- Fixed tracking sync to exclude decimals and respect chapter numbers (#63)
- Fixed UI login token retry and GQL wait
- Fixed settings export function registration (#63)
- Fixed recently-fetched data trigger for Recent Activity (#63)
- Fixed `system.rs` PathBuf error
- Disabled auto-complete on Moku inputs
- Biometric (Windows Hello) revision

## Chores
- Library folder settings revamp
- Rust cleanup follow-up

## Notes
- UI logic across some areas may not be fully clean yet — rough edges are expected
- Authentication is being actively reworked for smoother switching between servers and auth modes — currently in draft PR (#78) by zerebos, not yet merged
- Windows Hello is still under evaluation (#49)
- Touchscreen and gesture improvements ongoing (#29, #50)
- Proper database for Moku-Data is in progress (#56)

**Full Changelog:** https://github.com/moku-project/Moku/compare/v0.9.2...v0.9.3
